name: Preston
description_short: Preston Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving employees and families in Preston and Tucker counties since 1978, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards.
description_long:
Preston Federal Credit Union was established in 1978 and has spent nearly five decades serving members in Preston and Tucker counties, West Virginia. The institution positions itself as a member-owned credit union focused on personalized service and accessible financial products for its community. The credit union serves employees, volunteers, and family members from various businesses in the region, maintaining a local presence with a single branch in Kingwood, WV.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including share draft checking (checking accounts), multiple savings account options (regular savings, super saver, holiday clubs), CDs and IRA retirement accounts, consumer loans (auto, personal, motorcycle, RV, and share-secured), mortgage loans through a partnership with MyCUMortgage, student loans through Sallie Mae, and personal credit cards issued through MyCUCard with no annual fees. Members also have access to 24/7 online banking, mobile banking apps, and standard credit union amenities like payroll withholding.

What distinguishes Preston Federal is its deep local roots and community focus. The credit union actively supports its community through initiatives like the Robert F. Miller Memorial Scholarship and recently launched a First Annual Yard Sale to benefit local students. The institution emphasizes personal relationships and member ownership, contrasting with larger national banks. As a credit union, accounts are federally insured up to $250,000 by the NCUA.

The main limitation is geographic scope—Preston Federal serves a specific regional population in West Virginia and membership is restricted to eligible employees, volunteers, and family members of specific businesses. Those outside the eligible employment groups or geographic region cannot join. The single branch location limits in-person banking convenience for members outside the immediate Kingwood area, though online and mobile banking partially offset this constraint.
services: Traditional and Roth IRA retirement accounts
Student Loans through Sallie Mae partnership
MyCUCard personal credit cards with no annual fees
pros: Accounts federally insured up to $250,000 by NCUA
No annual fee credit cards with fraud protection and rewards through MyCUCard
24/7 online account access available free to members
Mobile banking apps available for iOS and Android
Diverse loan products including auto, personal, student, and mortgage options
Member-owned structure emphasizes community reinvestment and local scholarships
Established 1978—nearly 50 years of operational history and stability
cons: Membership restricted to employees, volunteers, and family members of specific businesses—not open to general public
Single branch location in Kingwood, WV limits physical banking access for members outside immediate area
Limited service area restricted to Preston and Tucker counties in West Virginia
Website does not disclose APR ranges or specific interest rates for loans and savings accounts
No mention of ATM network partnerships or surcharge-free ATM access for members
best_for: Employees and family members of eligible businesses in Preston and Tucker counties seeking local relationship banking
West Virginia residents in the service area looking for member-owned alternative to national banks
Members seeking basic checking, savings, and consumer loan products with community reinvestment
Borrowers interested in mortgage financing through credit union partnership options
diagnosis: Preston Federal Credit Union is best for employees and family members of eligible businesses in Preston and Tucker counties, West Virginia, seeking personalized relationship banking and community-focused credit union membership. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility restrictions—membership is not available to the general public and requires affiliation with specific eligible employers or families of members, making it inaccessible to most consumers outside its defined service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
